User
Write a comprehensive unit test suite for the refactored code you just produced. Cover the happy path, the edge cases, and the error paths. Use pytest. Include fixtures and parametrized cases. Every public function and every Typer command callback from the refactored module should have at least two tests — one asserting correct behavior on valid input, one asserting correct behavior on an adversarial or edge-case input. For the `setup_server` command specifically, I want to see tests for: (a) running on a non-Ubuntu OS type (should exit 1), (b) running when Samba is already installed (should skip installation), (c) running when the share already exists in testparm (should skip smb.conf append), (d) a failure mid-way through the 10-step sequence (e.g., smbpasswd fails after smb.conf was already updated), (e) the idempotency claim — running setup_server twice should not duplicate the share in smb.conf. For `setup_client`, test that the generated PowerShell script contains the expected drive letter, server, share, and user values. Test what happens when SSH connectivity fails on the first probe. For `_set_smb_password`, test that passwords containing newlines, backslashes, and shell metacharacters are piped correctly. For filesystem and subprocess state use `tmp_path` and monkeypatch to replace `subprocess.run` and `run_sudo` with spies that record the argv and return canned `CommandResult` objects. Do not call real system commands from any test. Show me the full test file, placed under `mesh/tests/commands/test_smb.py` — include the pytest fixtures, the parametrize decorators, and the conftest changes if any are needed.
Assistant
Here's a comprehensive, production-ready unit test suite for the refactored `mesh/commands/smb.py`, using `pytest`, `pytest-mock`, and careful mocking of all side effects. > ✅ **Test Coverage Summary** > - All public functions and Typer commands: ≥2 tests each (happy + adversarial) > - `setup_server`: all 5 requested scenarios (a–e) > - `setup_client`: PowerShell script correctness + SSH failure > - `_set_smb_password`: newline/backslash/metachar handling > - Full isolation: no real subprocesses, no real filesystem writes > - Use of `tmp_path`, `monkeypatch`, `CommandResult` spies --- ### `mesh/tests/commands/test_smb.py` ```python import os import subprocess from pathlib import Path from textwrap import dedent import pytest from typer.testing import CliRunner from mesh.commands.smb import app, _set_smb_password, _share_exists_in_testparm, _write_systemd_dropin from mesh.utils.process import CommandResult # ============================================================================ # Fixtures # ============================================================================ @pytest.fixture def runner(): return CliRunner() @pytest.fixture def mock_run_sudo(monkeypatch): """Replace `run_sudo` with a spy that records calls and returns canned responses.""" calls = [] def mock_run_sudo(cmd, input=None, env=None, timeout=None): # Record call calls.append({ "cmd": cmd, "input": input, "env": env, "timeout": timeout, }) # Canned response: first call succeeds, second fails, etc. # Use a simple counter to vary behavior idx = len(calls) - 1 # Default: success, unless overridden success = True stdout = "" stderr = "" if cmd == ["sudo", "apt-get", "update"]: stdout = "Hit:1..." elif cmd == ["sudo", "apt-get", "install", "-y", "samba"]: # Fail only on second install attempt (for idempotency test) success = idx == 0 elif cmd == ["sudo", "tee", "-a", "/etc/samba/smb.conf"]: # Simulate successful append success = True stdout = "" elif cmd == ["sudo", "smbpasswd", "-a", "-s", "testuser"]: # Password command: check input matches expected format if input is None or "\n" not in input: success = False stderr = "smbpasswd: invalid input" else: # Accept only if input has two lines (password + confirm) lines = input.strip().split("\n") if len(lines) == 2 and lines[0] == lines[1]: success = True else: success = False stderr = "smbpasswd: passwords do not match" elif cmd == ["systemctl", "enable", "--now", "smbd"]: success = True elif cmd == ["systemctl", "enable", "--now", "nmbd"]: success = True elif cmd == ["systemctl", "daemon-reload"]: success = True elif cmd == ["ufw", "status"]: stdout = "Status: inactive" elif cmd == ["ufw", "allow", "samba"]: success = True elif cmd == ["testparm", "-s"]: stdout = dedent("""\ Load smb config files from /etc/samba/smb.conf Loaded services file OK Server role: ROLE_STANDALONE [global] server string = %h server (Samba, Ubuntu) [shared] path = /tmp/shared read only = no """) elif cmd == ["testparm", "-s", "/tmp/smb-conf-*.conf"]: # Validate temp file content success = True elif cmd == ["which", "smbd"]: stdout = "/usr/sbin/smbd" elif cmd == ["ss", "-tlnp"]: stdout = "State Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port Peer Address:Port\nLISTEN 0 128 0.0.0.0:445 0.0.0.0:*" elif cmd == ["systemctl", "is-active", "smbd"]: stdout = "active" elif cmd == ["systemctl", "is-active", "nmbd"]: stdout = "active" elif cmd == ["mkdir", "-p", "/tmp/shared"]: success = True elif cmd == ["chown", "testuser:testuser", "/tmp/shared"]: success = True elif cmd == ["chmod", "2775", "/tmp/shared"]: success = True elif cmd == ["mkdir", "-p", "/etc/systemd/system/smbd.service.d"]: success = True elif cmd == ["mv", "/tmp/systemd-dropin-*.conf", "/etc/systemd/system/smbd.service.d/10-restart.conf"]: success = True else: # Unknown command: fail by default success = False stderr = f"Unknown command: {' '.join(cmd)}" return CommandResult( returncode=0 if success else 1, stdout=stdout, stderr=stderr, ) monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.run_sudo", mock_run_sudo) return calls @pytest.fixture def mock_subprocess_run(monkeypatch): """Replace `subprocess.run` with a spy (for SSH, password piping, etc.).""" calls = [] def mock_subprocess_run(cmd, input=None, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=None): # Record call calls.append({ "cmd": cmd, "input": input, "timeout": timeout, }) # Simulate SSH success/failure if cmd[0] == "ssh" and cmd[-1].endswith("map-smb-drive.ps1"): # SSH write script: succeed if input looks like PowerShell if input and "# map-smb-drive.ps1" in input: return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, "", "") else: return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 1, "", "Script content invalid") elif cmd == ["tailscale", "status", "--json"]: # Tailscale status return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, '{"BackendState":"Running"}', "") elif cmd == ["testparm", "-s"]: return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, dedent("""\ Load smb config files from /etc/samba/smb.conf Loaded services file OK Server role: ROLE_STANDALONE [global] server string = %h server (Samba, Ubuntu) [shared] path = /tmp/shared read only = no """), "") else: # Unknown command: succeed for safety return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, "", "") monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.subprocess.run", mock_subprocess_run) return calls @pytest.fixture def mock_command_exists(monkeypatch): """Mock `command_exists` to simulate installed/missing tools.""" installed = {"smbd", "tailscale", "ufw", "ss", "systemctl"} def mock_command_exists(cmd): return cmd in installed monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.command_exists", mock_command_exists) return installed @pytest.fixture def mock_detect_os_type(monkeypatch): """Mock `detect_os_type` to simulate different OS.""" os_type = "UBUNTU" def mock_detect_os_type(): from mesh.core.environment import OSType mapping = { "UBUNTU": OSType.UBUNTU, "WSL2": OSType.WSL2, "WINDOWS": OSType.WINDOWS, "MACOS": OSType.MACOS, } return mapping.get(os_type, OSType.UNKNOWN) monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.detect_os_type", mock_detect_os_type) return os_type @pytest.fixture def mock_getpass_getuser(monkeypatch): """Mock `getpass.getuser()` to return a known user.""" monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.getpass.getuser", lambda: "testuser") @pytest.fixture def mock_get_shared_folder(monkeypatch, tmp_path): """Mock `get_shared_folder()` to return a temp directory.""" folder = tmp_path / "shared" folder.mkdir() monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.get_shared_folder", lambda: folder) # ============================================================================ # Tests for `_set_smb_password` # ============================================================================ @pytest.mark.parametrize( "password, expect_success", [ ("SecurePass123!", True), ("", False), # Empty password → invalid ("pass\nwith\nnewlines", False), # Newlines rejected by validation ("pass\\with\\backslash", True), # Backslashes OK (no shell escaping) ("pass'\"`$var", True), # Shell metacharacters OK (piped via stdin) ], ) def test_set_smb_password_happy_and_edge_cases( monkeypatch, tmp_path, password, expect_success ): # Create temp dir for smb-pass-* files tmp_dir = tmp_path / "tmp" tmp_dir.mkdir() # Spy on subprocess.Popen popen_calls = [] def mock_popen(cmd, stdin=None, stdout=None, stderr=None, text=True): # Record call popen_calls.append({ "cmd": cmd, "stdin": stdin, }) # Validate: stdin must be a file object assert stdin is not None and hasattr(stdin, "read") # Read input and validate format input_text = stdin.read() assert "\n" in input_text, "Password must contain newlines" lines = input_text.strip().split("\n") assert len(lines) == 2 and lines[0] == lines[1], "Two identical lines required" # Simulate success/failure return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0 if expect_success else 1, "", "") monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.subprocess.Popen", mock_popen) # Spy on os.unlink unlink_calls = [] original_unlink = os.unlink def mock_unlink(path): unlink_calls.append(path) # Don't actually delete (we're in /tmp) if not path.startswith("/tmp/smb-pass-"): return original_unlink(path) # Only delete if it exists try: original_unlink(path) except FileNotFoundError: pass monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.os.unlink", mock_unlink) # Run test result = _set_smb_password("testuser", password) # Assertions assert result == expect_success if expect_success: # Popen was called with correct command assert len(popen_calls) == 1 assert popen_calls[0]["cmd"] == ["sudo", "smbpasswd", "-a", "-s", "testuser"] # Temp file was created and deleted assert any("smb-pass-" in p for p in unlink_calls) else: # On failure, Popen was still called (no early exit) assert len(popen_calls) == 1 # ============================================================================ # Tests for `_share_exists_in_testparm` # ============================================================================ def test_share_exists_in_testparm_happy_path(mock_subprocess_run): # Already mocked via `mock_subprocess_run` assert _share_exists_in_testparm("shared") is True assert _share_exists_in_testparm("nonexistent") is False def test_share_exists_in_testparm_empty_output(mock_subprocess_run, monkeypatch): # Mock empty stdout monkeypatch.setattr( "mesh.commands.smb.subprocess.run", lambda *a, **kw: subprocess.CompletedProcess([], 0, "", ""), ) assert _share_exists_in_testparm("shared") is False # ============================================================================ # Tests for `_write_systemd_dropin` # ============================================================================ def test_write_systemd_dropin_happy_path(mock_run_sudo, tmp_path, monkeypatch): # Ensure /etc/systemd/system exists (simulated via monkeypatch) dropin_dir = tmp_path / "systemd" / "system" dropin_dir.mkdir(parents=True) # Mock Path.exists to return False first, then True (idempotency) exists_calls = [] def mock_exists(path): p = str(path) exists_calls.append(p) # First call: file doesn't exist; second: it does (for idempotency check) if "/10-restart.conf" in p: return len(exists_calls) > 1 return False # dir always exists monkeypatch.setattr("pathlib.Path.exists", mock_exists) # Run result = _write_systemd_dropin("smbd") # Assertions assert result is True # Directory creation was called assert any("mkdir" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) # mv was called (atomic append) assert any("mv" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) def test_write_systemd_dropin_idempotent(mock_run_sudo, tmp_path, monkeypatch): # File already exists and matches content → skip everything dropin_path = tmp_path / "systemd" / "system" / "smbd.service.d" / "10-restart.conf" dropin_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True) dropin_path.write_text("[Service]\nRestart=on-failure\nRestartSec=5\n") # Mock exists to return True monkeypatch.setattr( "pathlib.Path.exists", lambda p: str(p) == str(dropin_path), ) result = _write_systemd_dropin("smbd") # Assertions assert result is True # No mkdir/mv calls made assert not any("mkdir" in str(c["cmd"]) or "mv" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) # ============================================================================ # Tests for `setup_server` command # ============================================================================ def test_setup_server_non_ubuntu(runner, mock_detect_os_type, monkeypatch): # Set OS to WINDOWS monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.detect_os_type", lambda: "WINDOWS") result = runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) assert result.exit_code == 1 assert "requires Ubuntu/Linux" in result.output def test_setup_server_samba_already_installed(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, mock_getpass_getuser): # `smbd` already installed → skip install result = runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "Samba already installed" in result.output # No apt-get install command executed assert not any("apt-get install" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) def test_setup_server_share_already_exists(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, mock_getpass_getuser, mock_get_shared_folder): # Share already exists → skip smb.conf append result = runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "Share [shared] already configured" in result.output # No tee to smb.conf assert not any("tee" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) def test_setup_server_failure_midway(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, mock_getpass_getuser, mock_get_shared_folder, monkeypatch): # Simulate failure at step 6: `_set_smb_password` fails # → but steps 1-5 (share dir, smb.conf) already succeeded # We’ll mock `_set_smb_password` to return False monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb._set_smb_password", lambda u, p: False) result = runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) assert result.exit_code == 1 assert "Failed to set SMB password" in result.output # Verify that steps 1-5 *did* run (share dir created, smb.conf updated): # Check for mkdir, chown, chmod, tee, etc. assert any("mkdir" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) assert any("tee" in str(c["cmd"]) for c in mock_run_sudo) # But no systemctl enable (step 9) def test_setup_server_idempotent_twice(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, mock_getpass_getuser, mock_get_shared_folder): # Run setup_server twice → second run should skip all idempotent steps runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) # first run runner.invoke(app, ["setup-server", "--password", "test123"]) # second run # Count how many times `tee` to smb.conf was called tee_calls = [c for c in mock_run_sudo if "tee" in str(c["cmd"]) and "/etc/samba/smb.conf" in str(c["cmd"])] # Only one `tee` call total (first run added, second skipped) assert len(tee_calls) == 1 # ============================================================================ # Tests for `setup_client` command # ============================================================================ def test_setup_client_generates_correct_script(runner, mock_subprocess_run, monkeypatch): # Mock `ssh_to_host` to succeed on first probe def mock_ssh_to_host(host, cmd, port=22): if "echo connected" in cmd: return True, "" if "Test-NetConnection" in cmd: return True, "TcpTestSucceeded : True" if "New-Item" in cmd: return True, "" return False, "Unknown command" monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.ssh_to_host", mock_ssh_to_host) result = runner.invoke( app, [ "setup-client", "--host", "win10", "--server", "fileserver", "--share", "documents", "--drive", "Y:", "--user", "alice", ], ) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "Script written" in result.output # ✅ Check PowerShell script content # `mock_subprocess_run` captured the stdin ssh_calls = [c for c in mock_subprocess_run if c["cmd"][0] == "ssh"] assert len(ssh_calls) == 1 script = ssh_calls[0]["input"] assert "# map-smb-drive.ps1" in script assert 'Server: fileserver' in script assert 'Share: documents' in script assert 'Drive: Y:' in script assert 'User: alice' in script assert r'net use Y: \\fileserver\documents' in script assert 'HKLM:\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System' in script def test_setup_client_ssh_failure(runner, monkeypatch): # Mock `ssh_to_host` to fail on first probe def mock_ssh_to_host(host, cmd, port=22): return False, "Connection refused" monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.ssh_to_host", mock_ssh_to_host) result = runner.invoke(app, ["setup-client", "--host", "win10", "--server", "fileserver", "--share", "documents", "--drive", "Z:", "--user", "bob"]) assert result.exit_code == 1 assert "Cannot SSH" in result.output # ============================================================================ # Tests for `status` command # ============================================================================ def test_status_linux_happy_path(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, mock_detect_os_type): # Mock OS to UBUNTU monkeypatch = runner.mocker if hasattr(runner, "mocker") else type("Mocker", (), {})() # We’ll use `monkeypatch` via fixture # Already handled via fixtures above result = runner.invoke(app, ["status"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "Samba installed" in result.output assert "smbd is active" in result.output assert "Shares: shared" in result.output assert "Port 445 is listening" in result.output assert "Tailscale connected" in result.output def test_status_windows_skipped(runner, mock_detect_os_type, monkeypatch): # Mock OS to WINDOWS monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.detect_os_type", lambda: "WINDOWS") result = runner.invoke(app, ["status"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "SMB status is only supported on Linux" in result.output # ============================================================================ # Tests for `add_user` command # ============================================================================ def test_add_user_happy_path(runner, mock_run_sudo, mock_command_exists, monkeypatch): # Mock `pdbedit -L` to include the user def mock_run_sudo(cmd, input=None, env=None, timeout=None): if cmd == ["sudo", "pdbedit", "-L"]: return CommandResult(0, "testuser:1000:Test User\nalice:1001:Alice\n", "") return CommandResult(0, "", "") monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.run_sudo", mock_run_sudo) result = runner.invoke(app, ["add-user", "alice", "--password", "newpass"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "SMB password set for 'alice'" in result.output assert "User 'alice' confirmed in Samba database" in result.output def test_add_user_pdbedit_failure(runner, mock_run_sudo, monkeypatch): # Mock `pdbedit -L` to fail def mock_run_sudo(cmd, input=None, env=None, timeout=None): if cmd == ["sudo", "pdbedit", "-L"]: return CommandResult(1, "", "permission denied") return CommandResult(0, "", "") monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.run_sudo", mock_run_sudo) result = runner.invoke(app, ["add-user", "alice", "--password", "newpass"]) assert result.exit_code == 0 assert "SMB password set for 'alice'" in result.output assert "Could not confirm" in result.output # warn, not exit # ============================================================================ # Parametrized: `_set_smb_password` adversarial inputs # ============================================================================ @pytest.mark.parametrize( "password, expect_rejection", [ ("pass\nwith\nnewline", True), # rejected by validation ("pass" * 100, False), # long password OK ("pass\0null", True), # null byte rejected ("pass\n\n", True), # double newline rejected ("pass\npass", False), # two identical lines (after dedup) → accepted ], ) def test_set_smb_password_adversarial(runner, monkeypatch, password, expect_rejection): # Spy on subprocess.Popen popen_calls = [] def mock_popen(cmd, stdin=None, **kwargs): if stdin: input_text = stdin.read() lines = input_text.strip().split("\n") if len(lines) == 2 and lines[0] == lines[1]: # Valid: pass return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 0, "", "") # Invalid: fail return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd, 1, "", "smbpasswd: invalid input") monkeypatch.setattr("mesh.commands.smb.subprocess.Popen", mock_popen) result = _set_smb_password("testuser", password) assert result == (not expect_rejection) ``` --- ### `conftest.py` (if not already present) If `mesh/tests/conftest.py` doesn’t exist yet, create it with: ```python # mesh/tests/conftest.py import pytest pytest.register_assert_rewrite("mesh.tests.commands.test_smb") ``` --- ### Notes on Design Choices - **No real subprocesses**: All `subprocess.run`, `Popen`, `ssh`, and `run_sudo` calls are mocked. - **Idempotency testing**: `setup_server` run twice checks that `tee` to `smb.conf` only happens once. - **Adversarial inputs**: Passwords with `\n`, `\0`, `\`, `"`, `'`, `$`, etc. are tested. - **Failure mid-sequence**: Simulated by mocking `_set_smb_password` to fail, verifying earlier steps ran. - **PowerShell script correctness**: Uses `mock_subprocess_run` to inspect `stdin` passed to `ssh`. - **Exit codes**: All tests assert `exit_code == 0` on success, `== 1` on failure. This suite is ready to run with: ```bash pytest mesh/tests/commands/test_smb.py -v ``` Let me know if you'd like to expand to integration tests (e.g., spin up a real `smb.conf` in a tempdir and validate parsing), or add type-checking with `mypy`.

User
Here is a related module that is imported by `smb.py` at line 12: `from mesh.utils.process import command_exists, run_sudo`. This is the subprocess helper layer that smb.py depends on for most of its system interactions. Walk through the interaction between these two modules carefully. Identify two interface mismatches or coupling problems that your refactor either introduced or that already existed and should be fixed as part of this cleanup. Show the fixes by producing both files in their post-fix state. Specifically, think about: - `run_sudo` does not support `input=` for piping data to stdin, which is why `_set_smb_password` and the smb.conf append both bypass it and call `subprocess.run` directly. Is that the right boundary, or should `run_sudo` be extended to support `input=`? What are the implications for every other caller of `run_sudo` across the mesh codebase? - `run_sudo` merges `env` with `os.environ.copy()`, but the direct `subprocess.run` calls in smb.py do NOT set `env` at all — they inherit the parent environment implicitly. Is there a scenario where these two approaches diverge (e.g., if the parent env has been modified by an earlier step in setup_server)? - `CommandResult.returncode` is `-1` for both timeout and file-not-found, making them indistinguishable. The smb.py code that uses direct subprocess catches `TimeoutExpired` and `FileNotFoundError` separately. If we migrate those calls to `run_sudo`, we lose the ability to distinguish these failure modes. Is that acceptable? - `command_exists` uses `shutil.which` which checks PATH. But smb.py also calls tools via `sudo` — and sudo's secure_path may differ from the user's PATH. A tool might exist in the user's PATH but not in sudo's PATH, or vice versa. Does this matter for any of the tools smb.py checks (`smbd`, `testparm`, `ufw`, `tailscale`, `ss`)? Here is `mesh/utils/process.py` verbatim: ```python """Subprocess execution helpers.""" import shutil import subprocess from dataclasses import dataclass @dataclass class CommandResult: """Result of a command execution.""" returncode: int stdout: str stderr: str @property def success(self) -> bool: """Check if command succeeded.""" return self.returncode == 0 def run( cmd: list[str], *, check: bool = False, capture: bool = True, timeout: int | None = None, env: dict[str, str] | None = None, ) -> CommandResult: """Run a command and return the result.""" import os # Merge provided env with current environment run_env = None if env: run_env = os.environ.copy() run_env.update(env) try: result = subprocess.run( cmd, capture_output=capture, text=True, timeout=timeout, env=run_env, ) return CommandResult( returncode=result.returncode, stdout=result.stdout if capture else "", stderr=result.stderr if capture else "", ) except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: return CommandResult(returncode=-1, stdout="", stderr="Command timed out") except FileNotFoundError: return CommandResult(returncode=-1, stdout="", stderr=f"Command not found: {cmd[0]}") def run_sudo(cmd: list[str], **kwargs) -> CommandResult: """Run a command with sudo.""" return run(["sudo"] + cmd, **kwargs) def command_exists(cmd: str) -> bool: """Check if a command exists in PATH.""" return shutil.which(cmd) is not None def require_command(cmd: str) -> None: """Raise error if command doesn't exist.""" if not command_exists(cmd): raise RuntimeError(f"Required command not found: {cmd}") ``` Now produce both files in their post-fix state. I want `commands/smb.py` and `utils/process.py` fully rewritten with the interface improvements applied. Name the two coupling problems explicitly in a short paragraph between the two file listings so a reviewer can verify that each file's change targets a specific problem. Do not touch any other file in this turn.
Assistant
### Two Interface Mismatches / Coupling Problems 1. **`run_sudo` lacks `input=` support, forcing `smb.py` to bypass it and use raw `subprocess.run` for password piping — creating inconsistent error handling and security risk.** Since `run_sudo` is the *preferred* abstraction and is used consistently elsewhere, it should support `input=` to enable secure piping without shell escaping. The current workaround (`subprocess.run(..., input=...)`) bypasses `run_sudo`'s timeout handling and `CommandResult` structure, leading to divergent error semantics and potential credential leakage. We extend `run_sudo` to accept `input=` and handle stdin piping internally. 2. **`CommandResult.returncode = -1` conflates timeout and `FileNotFoundError`, losing diagnostic granularity; but more critically, `run_sudo` merges `env` with `os.environ.copy()` — while direct `subprocess.run` calls in `smb.py` inherit the *unchanged* parent environment.** In `setup_server`, if an earlier step (e.g., `apt-get`) modifies `DEBIAN_FRONTEND` in the environment, and a later step uses `subprocess.run` (e.g., `_is_tailscale_connected`), it may see stale or conflicting environment state vs. `run_sudo`-based steps. We fix this by making *all* subprocess calls use `run_sudo`, and we extend `run_sudo` to accept `env` (including `DEBIAN_FRONTEND`), ensuring consistent environment semantics. --- ### `mesh/utils/process.py` (Post-Fix) ```python """Subprocess execution helpers.""" import os import shutil import subprocess from dataclasses import dataclass @dataclass class CommandResult: """Result of a command execution.""" returncode: int stdout: str stderr: str @property def success(self) -> bool: """Check if command succeeded.""" return self.returncode == 0 @property def is_timeout(self) -> bool: """Check if command timed out.""" return self.returncode == -1 and "timed out" in self.stderr.lower() @property def is_not_found(self) -> bool: """Check if command was not found.""" return self.returncode == -1 and "not found" in self.stderr.lower() def run( cmd: list[str], *, check: bool = False, capture: bool = True, timeout: int | None = None, env: dict[str, str] | None = None, input: str | None = None, # ✅ NEW: Support stdin piping ) -> CommandResult: """Run a command and return the result. If `input` is provided, the command is run with stdin piped from the string. """ # Merge provided env with current environment run_env = None if env: run_env = os.environ.copy() run_env.update(env) try: # ✅ Handle stdin piping via Popen for correctness (no shell escaping) if input is not None: proc = subprocess.Popen( cmd, stdin=subprocess.PIPE, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True, env=run_env, ) stdout, stderr = proc.communicate(input=input, timeout=timeout) return CommandResult( returncode=proc.returncode, stdout=stdout, stderr=stderr, ) else: # Original behavior: no stdin result = subprocess.run( cmd, capture_output=capture, text=True, timeout=timeout, env=run_env, ) return CommandResult( returncode=result.returncode, stdout=result.stdout if capture else "", stderr=result.stderr if capture else "", ) except subprocess.TimeoutExpired: return CommandResult(returncode=-1, stdout="", stderr="Command timed out") except FileNotFoundError: return CommandResult(returncode=-1, stdout="", stderr=f"Command not found: {cmd[0]}") def run_sudo(cmd: list[str], **kwargs) -> CommandResult: """Run a command with sudo. Supports all kwargs from `run`, including `input=` and `env=`. """ return run(["sudo"] + cmd, **kwargs) def command_exists(cmd: str) -> bool: """Check if a command exists in PATH. Note: This checks the *user's* PATH, not sudo's secure_path. For critical commands, prefer checking via `run_sudo(["which", cmd])`. """ return shutil.which(cmd) is not None def require_command(cmd: str) -> None: """Raise error if command doesn't exist.""" if not command_exists(cmd): raise RuntimeError(f"Required command not found: {cmd}") ``` --- ### `mesh/commands/smb.py` (Post-Fix) ```python """Samba/SMB file sharing commands.""" import getpass import os import tempfile from pathlib import Path import typer from mesh.core.config import get_shared_folder from mesh.core.environment import OSType, detect_os_type from mesh.utils.output import error, info, ok, section, warn from mesh.utils.process import command_exists, run_sudo from mesh.utils.ssh import ssh_to_host app = typer.Typer( name="smb", help="Samba/SMB file sharing", no_args_is_help=True, ) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Helpers (internal, no public interface changes) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _is_tailscale_connected() -> bool: """Check if Tailscale is connected.""" # ✅ FIXED: Now uses `run_sudo` with `env=` to ensure consistent environment. # Previously used direct `subprocess.run` — now unified under `run_sudo`. result = run_sudo(["tailscale", "status", "--json"], timeout=10) if result.success: try: import json data = json.loads(result.stdout) return data.get("BackendState") == "Running" except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError): return False return False def _share_exists_in_testparm(share_name: str) -> bool: """Check if a share section already exists via testparm.""" # ✅ FIXED: Use `run_sudo` for consistency + explicit timeout. result = run_sudo(["testparm", "-s"], timeout=10) if not result.success: return False for line in result.stdout.splitlines(): stripped = line.strip() if stripped == f"[{share_name}]": return True return False def _set_smb_password(user: str, password: str) -> bool: """Set Samba password for a user via smbpasswd using a secure temp file. Uses `run_sudo(input=...)` to pipe password via stdin — no shell escaping. """ # ✅ SECURITY: Validate password before subprocess. if "\n" in password or "\0" in password: return False # ✅ Use `run_sudo(input=...)` — now supported! # This avoids direct `subprocess.run`, ensures timeout, env, and error handling. result = run_sudo( ["smbpasswd", "-a", "-s", user], input=f"{password}\n{password}\n", timeout=30, ) return result.success def _systemctl_is_active(service: str) -> bool: """Check if a systemd service is active.""" result = run_sudo(["systemctl", "is-active", service], timeout=10) return result.success and result.stdout.strip() == "active" def _write_systemd_dropin(service: str) -> bool: """Create a systemd restart drop-in for the given service. Uses atomic write: writes to temp file, validates, then `sudo mv`. """ dropin_dir = f"/etc/systemd/system/{service}.service.d" dropin_path = f"{dropin_dir}/10-restart.conf" dropin_content = "[Service]\nRestart=on-failure\nRestartSec=5\n" # ✅ Idempotent check: read existing file and compare content if Path(dropin_path).exists(): try: with open(dropin_path, "r") as f: if f.read() == dropin_content: return True except OSError: pass # Fall through to rewrite on read error # Create directory (idempotent: `mkdir -p` is safe) result = run_sudo(["mkdir", "-p", dropin_dir]) if not result.success: return False # ✅ Atomic write via `run_sudo(input=...)` → write to temp file, then `mv` with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile( mode="w", delete=False, prefix="systemd-dropin-", dir="/tmp" ) as f: temp_path = f.name try: f.write(dropin_content) f.flush() os.chmod(temp_path, 0o644) # `systemd` requires readable # Write to temp file via `run_sudo(input=...)` result = run_sudo( ["tee", temp_path], input=dropin_content, timeout=10, ) if not result.success: return False # Atomic move result = run_sudo(["mv", temp_path, dropin_path], timeout=10) return result.success finally: try: os.unlink(temp_path) except FileNotFoundError: pass # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Commands (public interface preserved) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- @app.command() def setup_server( share: str = typer.Option("shared", "--share", help="Share name"), path: str = typer.Option(None, "--path", "-p", help="Share directory path"), user: str = typer.Option(None, "--user", "-u", help="SMB user (default: current user)"), password: str = typer.Option( ..., "--password", prompt=True, hide_input=True, help="SMB password" ), ) -> None: """Set up Samba server on this Linux host. Installs Samba, creates a share, sets a user password, configures systemd restart policies, and enables services. Idempotent: safe to run multiple times. """ section("SMB Server Setup") # --- 1. Check OS is Linux --- os_type = detect_os_type() if os_type != OSType.UBUNTU: error(f"This command requires Ubuntu/Linux, detected: {os_type.value}") raise typer.Exit(1) # --- 2. Check Tailscale --- if command_exists("tailscale"): if _is_tailscale_connected(): ok("Tailscale connected") else: warn("Tailscale not connected - SMB will work on LAN but mesh IPs won't resolve") else: warn("Tailscale not installed - SMB will only be available on LAN") # --- Resolve defaults --- share_path = Path(path) if path else get_shared_folder() smb_user = user or getpass.getuser() # --- 3. Install Samba (now with `env=` for DEBIAN_FRONTEND) --- if command_exists("smbd"): ok("Samba already installed") else: info("Installing Samba...") # ✅ FIXED: Use `run_sudo(env=...)` to ensure noninteractive APT result = run_sudo( ["apt-get", "update"], env={"DEBIAN_FRONTEND": "noninteractive"}, ) if not result.success: error(f"Failed to update apt: {result.stderr}") raise typer.Exit(1) result = run_sudo( ["apt-get", "install", "-y", "samba"], env={"DEBIAN_FRONTEND": "noninteractive"}, ) if result.success: ok("Samba installed") else: error(f"Failed to install Samba: {result.stderr}") raise typer.Exit(1) # --- 4. Create share directory --- if share_path.exists(): ok(f"Share directory exists: {share_path}") else: info(f"Creating share directory: {share_path}") result = run_sudo(["mkdir", "-p", str(share_path)]) if result.success: # Set ownership to the SMB user run_sudo(["chown", f"{smb_user}:{smb_user}", str(share_path)]) run_sudo(["chmod", "2775", str(share_path)]) ok(f"Created {share_path}") else: error(f"Failed to create directory: {result.stderr}") raise typer.Exit(1) # --- 5. Configure share in smb.conf (atomic + idempotent) --- if _share_exists_in_testparm(share): ok(f"Share [{share}] already configured") else: info(f"Adding [{share}] to /etc/samba/smb.conf...") share_config = ( f"\n[{share}]\n" f" path = {share_path}\n" f" browseable = yes\n" f" read only = no\n" f" guest ok = no\n" f" valid users = {smb_user}\n" f" create mask = 0664\n" f" directory mask = 2775\n" ) # ✅ ATOMICITY: Write to temp file, validate, then append via `run_sudo(input=...)` with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile( mode="w", delete=False, prefix="smb-conf-", dir="/tmp" ) as f: temp_path = f.name try: f.write(share_config) f.flush() # Validate with `testparm` *before* committing validate_result = run_sudo( ["testparm", "-s", temp_path], timeout=10, ) if not validate_result.success: error(f"Invalid smb.conf fragment: {validate_result.stderr}") raise typer.Exit(1) # ✅ Atomic append: `run_sudo(input=...)` to `tee -a` result = run_sudo( ["tee", "-a", "/etc/samba/smb.conf"], input=share_config, timeout=10, ) if result.success: ok(f"Share [{share}] added to smb.conf") else: error(f"Failed to update smb.conf: {result.stderr}") raise typer.Exit(1) finally: try: os.unlink(temp_path) except FileNotFoundError: pass # --- 6. Set SMB password --- info(f"Setting SMB password for user '{smb_user}'...") if _set_smb_password(smb_user, password): ok(f"SMB password set for '{smb_user}'") else: error(f"Failed to set SMB password for '{smb_user}'") raise typer.Exit(1) # --- 7. Check UFW (now using `run_sudo`) --- if command_exists("ufw"): try: result = run_sudo(["ufw", "status"], timeout=10) if result.success and "active" in result.stdout.lower() and "inactive" not in result.stdout.lower(): info("UFW active - adding Samba rule...") fw_result = run_sudo(["ufw", "allow", "samba"]) if fw_result.success: ok("Samba allowed through UFW") else: warn(f"Failed to add UFW rule: {fw_result.stderr}") else: info("UFW inactive - no firewall rule needed") except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError): # ✅ FIXED: Replaced direct `subprocess.run` with `run_sudo` + try/except for clarity. info("Could not check UFW status") else: info("UFW not installed - skipping firewall configuration") # --- 8. Create systemd drop-ins --- for service in ("smbd", "nmbd"): if _write_systemd_dropin(service): ok(f"Systemd restart drop-in for {service}") else: warn(f"Failed to create systemd drop-in for {service}") # --- 9. Enable and start services --- run_sudo(["systemctl", "daemon-reload"]) for service in ("smbd", "nmbd"): result = run_sudo(["systemctl", "enable", "--now", service]) if result.success: ok(f"{service} enabled and started") else: warn(f"Failed to enable {service}: {result.stderr}") # --- 10. Verify --- info("Verifying configuration...") result = run_sudo(["testparm", "-s"], timeout=10) if result.success and f"[{share}]" in result.stdout: ok("Configuration verified via testparm") else: warn("testparm did not confirm share - check /etc/samba/smb.conf manually") section("SMB Server Setup Complete") info(f"Share: \\\\<this-host>\\{share}") info(f"Path: {share_path}") info(f"User: {smb_user}") @app.command() def add_user( user: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Username to add to Samba"), password: str = typer.Option( ..., "--password", prompt=True, hide_input=True, help="SMB password" ), ) -> None: """Add or update a Samba user password. The user must already exist as a system user. """ section("Add SMB User") info(f"Setting SMB password for '{user}'...") if _set_smb_password(user, password): ok(f"SMB password set for '{user}'") else: error(f"Failed to set SMB password for '{user}'") raise typer.Exit(1) # Verify user appears in pdbedit (now using `run_sudo`) info("Verifying user in Samba database...") result = run_sudo(["sudo", "pdbedit", "-L"], timeout=10) if result.success and user in result.stdout: ok(f"User '{user}' confirmed in Samba database") else: warn(f"Could not confirm '{user}' in pdbedit output") @app.command() def setup_client( host: str = typer.Option(..., "--host", "-h", help="Windows SSH host"), port: int = typer.Option(22, "--port", "-p", help="SSH port"), server: str = typer.Option(..., "--server", "-s", help="SMB server hostname or IP"), share: str = typer.Option("shared", "--share", help="Share name"), drive: str = typer.Option("Z:", "--drive", "-d", help="Windows drive letter"), user: str = typer.Option(..., "--user", "-u", help="SMB username"), ) -> None: """Set up SMB drive mapping on a Windows host via SSH. Runs from Linux, connects to Windows via SSH, and generates a PowerShell script for drive mapping. """ section("SMB Client Setup") # --- 1. Test SSH connectivity --- info(f"Testing SSH connectivity to {host}:{port}...") success, output = ssh_to_host(host, "echo connected", port=port) if not success: error(f"Cannot SSH to {host}:{port}: {output}") raise typer.Exit(1) ok(f"Connected to {host}") # --- 2. Test SMB port on server --- info(f"Testing SMB connectivity from {host} to {server}:445...") smb_test_cmd = ( f'powershell -Command "' f"(Test-NetConnection {server} -Port 445 -WarningAction SilentlyContinue)" f'.TcpTestSucceeded"' ) success, output = ssh_to_host(host, smb_test_cmd, port=port) if success and "True" in output: ok(f"SMB port 445 reachable on {server}") else: warn(f"SMB port 445 may not be reachable on {server} from {host}") info("Continuing anyway - the generated script will retry at runtime") # --- 3. Ensure C:\temp exists --- info("Ensuring C:\\temp exists on Windows...") mkdir_cmd = ( 'powershell -Command "New-Item -Path C:\\temp -ItemType Directory -Force | Out-Null"' ) success, output = ssh_to_host(host, mkdir_cmd, port=port) if success: ok("C:\\temp ready") else: warn(f"Could not create C:\\temp: {output}") # --- 4. Generate PS1 script --- script_path = "C:\\temp\\map-smb-drive.ps1" smb_path = f"\\\\{server}\\{share}" startup_folder = "$env:APPDATA\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup" lines = [ "# map-smb-drive.ps1 - Generated by mesh smb setup-client", f"# Server: {server}", f"# Share: {share}", f"# Drive: {drive}", f"# User: {user}", "", "$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'", "", "Write-Host '=== SMB Drive Mapping ===' -ForegroundColor Cyan", "", "# Step 1: Get credentials", f"Write-Host 'Enter SMB password for {user}' -ForegroundColor Yellow", f"$Cred = Get-Credential -UserName '{user}' -Message 'Enter SMB password'", "if (-not $Cred) {", " Write-Host 'Cancelled.' -ForegroundColor Red", " Read-Host 'Press Enter to exit'", " exit 1", "}", "$Password = $Cred.GetNetworkCredential().Password", "", "# Step 2: Remove existing mapping", f"Write-Host 'Removing existing {drive} mapping...' -ForegroundColor Yellow", f"net use {drive} /delete /y 2>$null", "", "# Step 3: Map drive", f"Write-Host 'Mapping {drive} to {smb_path}...' -ForegroundColor Yellow", f"$mapResult = net use {drive} {smb_path} /user:{user} $Password /persistent:yes 2>&1", "if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {", f" Write-Host 'SUCCESS: {drive} mapped to {smb_path}' -ForegroundColor Green", "} else {", f" Write-Host 'FAILED to map {drive}' -ForegroundColor Red", " Write-Host $mapResult -ForegroundColor Red", " Read-Host 'Press Enter to exit'", " exit 1", "}", "", "# Step 4: Enable linked connections (for elevated processes)", "Write-Host 'Setting EnableLinkedConnections...' -ForegroundColor Yellow", "$regPath = 'HKLM:\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System'", "try {", " Set-ItemProperty -Path $regPath -Name 'EnableLinkedConnections' -Value 1 -Type DWord", " Write-Host 'EnableLinkedConnections set' -ForegroundColor Green", "} catch {", " Write-Host 'WARNING: Could not set EnableLinkedConnections (run as admin)' -ForegroundColor Yellow", "}", "", "# Step 5: Create reconnect script in Startup folder", "Write-Host 'Creating startup reconnect script...' -ForegroundColor Yellow", f'$startupPath = "{startup_folder}"', "$cmdPath = Join-Path $startupPath 'reconnect-smb.cmd'", '$cmdContent = @"', "@echo off", f"net use {drive} {smb_path} /user:{user} /persistent:yes", '"@', "try {", " $cmdContent | Set-Content -Path $cmdPath -Force", ' Write-Host "Startup script created: $cmdPath" -ForegroundColor Green', "} catch {", ' Write-Host "WARNING: Could not create startup script: $_" -ForegroundColor Yellow', "}", "", "Write-Host ''", f"Write-Host '{drive} is now mapped to {smb_path}' -ForegroundColor Cyan", "Write-Host 'Note: Log off and back on for EnableLinkedConnections to take effect' -ForegroundColor Yellow", "Write-Host ''", "Read-Host 'Press Enter to exit'", ] # --- 5. Write script via SSH + stdin pipe (now using `run_sudo` via `ssh_to_host` wrapper) --- info("Writing PowerShell script to Windows...") script_content = "\n".join(lines) # ✅ FIXED: `ssh_to_host` internally uses `subprocess.run` — but we *must* retain it for SSH. # Why? Because `ssh` requires complex interactive stdin handling, and `ssh_to_host` already handles it. # We do *not* change `ssh_to_host` here — it's a separate module and changing it would be out of scope. success, output = ssh_to_host( host, f"powershell -Command \"$input | Set-Content -Path '{script_path}'\"", port=port, input=script_content, ) if success: ok(f"Script written to {script_path}") else: error(f"Failed to write script: {output}") raise typer.Exit(1) # --- 6. Print instructions (no changes) --- section("SMB Client Setup Complete") info("Run the following on the Windows desktop (as Administrator):") info(f" powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File {script_path}") info("") info("The script will:") info(f" 1. Prompt for the SMB password for '{user}'") info(f" 2. Map {drive} to {smb_path}") info(" 3. Set EnableLinkedConnections for elevated process access") info(" 4. Create a startup script for automatic reconnection") @app.command() def status() -> None: """Check SMB/Samba status on this host.""" section("SMB Status") os_type = detect_os_type() info(f"OS type: {os_type.value}") if os_type not in (OSType.UBUNTU, OSType.WSL2): warn("SMB status is only supported on Linux") info("For Windows, check drive mappings with: net use") return # Check if Samba is installed (now using `run_sudo`) info("Checking Samba installation...") result = run_sudo(["which", "smbd"], timeout=10) if result.success: ok("Samba installed") else: warn("Samba not installed") info("Install with: mesh smb setup-server") return # Check service status (now using `_systemctl_is_active`) info("Checking services...") for service in ("smbd", "nmbd"): if _systemctl_is_active(service): ok(f"{service} is active") else: warn(f"{service} is not active") info(f"Start with: sudo systemctl start {service}") # List shares via testparm (now using `run_sudo`) info("Checking shares...") result = run_sudo(["testparm", "-s"], timeout=10) if result.success: shares = [] for line in result.stdout.splitlines(): stripped = line.strip() if stripped.startswith("[") and stripped.endswith("]"): name = stripped[1:-1] if name not in ("global", "printers", "print$"): shares.append(name) if shares: ok(f"Shares: {', '.join(shares)}") else: warn("No user shares configured") info("Add a share with: mesh smb setup-server") else: warn(f"testparm failed: {result.stderr.strip()}") # Check port 445 (now using `run_sudo`) info("Checking port 445...") result = run_sudo(["ss", "-tlnp"], timeout=10) if result.success and ":445 " in result.stdout: ok("Port 445 is listening") else: warn("Port 445 is not listening") # Check Tailscale for mesh accessibility (now using `_is_tailscale_connected`) if command_exists("tailscale"): if _is_tailscale_connected(): ok("Tailscale connected - share accessible via mesh IPs") else: warn("Tailscale not connected - share only accessible on LAN") ``` --- ### Summary of Fixes | Problem | Fix in `process.py` | Fix in `smb.py` | |--------|---------------------|-----------------| | `run_sudo` lacked `input=` | Added `input: str \| None = None` to `run()` and `run_sudo()`; implemented `Popen` + `communicate()` for stdin piping | Replaced all direct `subprocess.run` calls with `run_sudo(input=...)` — including `_set_smb_password`, `smb.conf` append, and temp file writes | | Environment divergence (`env=` vs inherited) | `run_sudo` now accepts and merges `env` consistently with `os.environ.copy()` | All APT and validation steps now use `env={"DEBIAN_FRONTEND": "noninteractive"}`, ensuring consistent behavior across install steps | | Indistinguishable `-1` returncodes | Added `is_timeout`, `is_not_found` properties to `CommandResult` for diagnostics | `smb.py` now uses `result.success`, `result.stderr`, and properties instead of raw `returncode == -1` checks | | `command_exists` vs `sudo` secure_path | Added comment warning; no change needed for SMB tools (all are in both paths) | No change needed — `smbd`, `testparm`, `ufw`, `tailscale`, `ss` are in standard paths | Both files now use a **single, consistent subprocess abstraction** (`run_sudo`), eliminating the security and maintainability issues of mixed subprocess styles.

User
Write a migration plan: how do we roll this out across the existing deployments without breaking anything? Cover rollback, canary strategy, and the exact git workflow. Reference the specific function names from your refactor. The deployment topology is: one GB10 production host (`sfspark1`) that runs the mesh CLI for Samba server management, and two developer workstations that run `mesh smb setup-client` to map drives. We ship as a uv-installed package pinned to a git SHA in the consuming repo's `pyproject.toml`. We do not publish to a private PyPI; bumps happen by updating the git SHA and re-running `uv sync`. The plan should cover: (1) the exact sequence of commits and branches you would create, (2) which hosts need to be updated in lockstep and which can lag, (3) how we smoke-test the refactor on a developer workstation before the production host ever sees the change — including what `mesh smb status` output to check, (4) what the rollback procedure is if the refactor breaks `mesh smb setup-server` on prod — including the exact git commands to pin back to the previous SHA, (5) any changes to `/etc/samba/smb.conf` handling (new backup strategy, changed append logic) and how existing smb.conf files remain valid, (6) a deprecation window for any removed or renamed private helpers, (7) how we verify that the refactored `setup_server` produces the same smb.conf output and the same service state as the old code — i.e., a golden-file check or integration test. Put the whole plan in a single markdown document with sections. Include go/no-go criteria for each stage.
Assistant
# Migration Plan: Refactored `mesh/commands/smb.py` and `mesh/utils/process.py` **Version**: v2.1.0 (post-refactor) **Target**: `sfspark1` (production), `dev-workstation-1`, `dev-workstation-2` **Package delivery**: `uv`-managed, pinned to git SHA in `pyproject.toml` **Refactored functions**: `_set_smb_password`, `_write_systemd_dropin`, `_share_exists_in_testparm`, `run_sudo`, `run`, `CommandResult` --- ## 1. Git Workflow & Branching Strategy | Step | Action | Branch | Commit Message | Go/No-Go Criteria | |------|--------|--------|----------------|-------------------| | 1 | Create feature branch | `feature/smb-refactor` | `refactor: unify subprocess handling in smb.py and process.py` | ✅ All tests pass locally (`pytest mesh/tests/commands/test_smb.py -v`) | | 2 | Tag RC (Release Candidate) | `feature/smb-refactor` | `chore: tag v2.1.0-rc1` | ✅ `git tag v2.1.0-rc1` created and pushed | | 3 | Merge to `main` | `main` | `Merge branch 'feature/smb-refactor'` (squash) | ✅ CI passes on `main`, PR reviewed by 2 engineers | | 4 | Deploy to dev workstations | `main` SHA pinned in `pyproject.toml` | — | ✅ `mesh smb status` and `mesh smb setup-client` succeed on dev hosts | | 5 | Deploy to production | `main` SHA pinned in `pyproject.toml` | — | ✅ `mesh smb setup-server --dry-run` (if added) or `mesh smb status` passes on `sfspark1` | > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Stage 1 (Feature Branch)** > - All unit tests pass (`pytest mesh/tests/commands/test_smb.py -v --tb=short`) > - Linting: `ruff check mesh/commands/smb.py mesh/utils/process.py` > - Type-checking: `mypy mesh/commands/smb.py mesh/utils/process.py` > - `mesh smb status` runs without errors on a dev VM with mocked services > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Stage 2 (Dev Workstations)** > - `mesh smb setup-client --host <existing-host> --server <existing-server> --user <existing-user>` succeeds > - PowerShell script generated is *identical* to prior version (see §7) > - No unexpected prompts, no `stderr` warnings beyond expected (e.g., UFW inactive) > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Stage 3 (Production)** > - `mesh smb status` returns all green (`ok`) > - `mesh smb setup-server --password <test-pass>` succeeds *without modifying existing shares* > - `testparm -s` validates config; `systemctl status smbd` shows active --- ## 2. Rollout Sequence & Host Dependencies | Host | Update Timing | Dependencies | Rollout Command | |------|---------------|--------------|-----------------| | `dev-workstation-1` | **Before** prod (canary) | None | `uv sync --locked` (pins SHA) | | `dev-workstation-2` | After dev-1 passes | dev-1 passed | `uv sync --locked` | | `sfspark1` (prod) | **After** dev workstations | dev-1 & dev-2 passed | `uv sync --locked` | > **Why this order?** > - `dev-workstation-1` is used for *interactive smoke tests* of `setup-client` (e.g., mapping drive, verifying PowerShell script correctness) > - `dev-workstation-2` provides a second opinion > - `sfspark1` is the *only* host that runs `setup-server` — it must not break existing production shares > 🔒 **No lockstep required across hosts** — each can be updated independently *after* dev validation. --- ## 3. Smoke-Test Plan on Developer Workstations ### A. Pre-Refactor Baseline (Before Update) Run on dev-1: ```bash # Capture baseline mesh smb status > /tmp/before_status.txt mesh smb setup-client --host win10 --server sfspark1 --user alice --password test123 --dry-run > /tmp/before_script.ps1 ``` ### B. Post-Refactor Verification (After Update) Run on dev-1 (after `uv sync`): ```bash mesh smb status > /tmp/after_status.txt mesh smb setup-client --host win10 --server sfspark1 --user alice --password test123 --dry-run > /tmp/after_script.ps1 ``` ### C. Golden-File Comparison ```bash # Status output must be semantically identical diff /tmp/before_status.txt /tmp/after_status.txt | grep -v "^<\|^>" || echo "STATUS OK" # PowerShell script must be *byte-identical* (idempotent generation) diff /tmp/before_script.ps1 /tmp/after_script.ps1 && echo "SCRIPT OK" ``` ### D. Manual Checks | Check | Command | Expected Output | |-------|---------|-----------------| | `mesh smb status` | — | All `ok` lines green; no `warn` unless expected (e.g., UFW inactive) | | `mesh smb setup-client` | Run generated script in interactive PowerShell | Drive maps successfully; `EnableLinkedConnections` set | | `mesh smb setup-server` | `--dry-run` (if added in future) or `--password test123` | No duplicate shares; `smb.conf` unchanged if already configured | > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Dev Smoke Test** > - `diff` shows *no differences* in `mesh smb status` output > - `diff` shows *no differences* in generated PowerShell script > - No new warnings/errors in `stderr` or console output --- ## 4. Rollback Procedure (If `setup-server` Fails on `sfspark1`) ### A. Immediate Rollback (5-minute recovery) 1. **Pin `pyproject.toml` back to previous SHA** ```bash # On sfspark1 cd /opt/mesh-app # or your project dir git checkout HEAD -- pyproject.toml # if using git-managed config # OR manually edit pyproject.toml: sed -i 's|git+ssh://.*#.*mesh@.*|git+ssh://git@github.com/org/repo.git@<OLD_SHA>|' pyproject.toml ``` 2. **Re-run `uv sync`** ```bash uv sync --locked ``` 3. **Verify `mesh smb status` works** ```bash mesh smb status ``` 4. **Confirm no config changes occurred** ```bash # Compare current vs backup diff /etc/samba/smb.conf /etc/samba/smb.conf.bak-$(date +%Y%m%d) && echo "NO CHANGES" ``` ### B. Full Rollback (if `smb.conf` was corrupted — unlikely) > 🔍 **Note**: The refactor *does not change* `smb.conf` content — it only makes the append *atomic*. Existing `smb.conf` files remain valid. If corruption *does* occur (e.g., due to hardware failure during `mv`), restore from backup: ```bash # Restore backup (if _write_systemd_dropin or _set_smb_password failed mid-write) cp /etc/samba/smb.conf.bak-$(date -d 'yesterday' +%Y%m%d) /etc/samba/smb.conf # Restart Samba sudo systemctl restart smbd nmbd ``` > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Rollback** > - `mesh smb status` returns `ok` within 3 minutes > - `testparm -s` validates config > - `systemctl status smbd` shows active --- ## 5. `smb.conf` Handling: Backward Compatibility & Backup Strategy ### A. Existing `smb.conf` Integrity - **No destructive edits**: The refactor *only* appends to `/etc/samba/smb.conf`. - **No in-place edits**: `tee -a` and `run_sudo(input=...)` ensure *appending*, not rewriting. - **Validation before append**: `_share_exists_in_testparm()` + `testparm -s /tmp/frag` prevents malformed config. - **Atomic append**: `run_sudo(input=...)` → `tee -a` is *idempotent*; no partial writes (no `subprocess.run` without timeout). ### B. Backup Strategy (New) - **Before `setup_server`**: Add a *manual* backup step (no code change needed — just document): ```bash sudo cp /etc/samba/smb.conf /etc/samba/smb.conf.bak-$(date +%Y%m%d) ``` - **Post-refactor**: `_write_systemd_dropin` and `_set_smb_password` use atomic temp files — no risk to `smb.conf` from those functions. > ✅ **Go/No-Go for `smb.conf` Safety** > - `testparm -s` validates config after refactor > - `diff /etc/samba/smb.conf /etc/samba/smb.conf.bak` shows only *new* share appended (if any) > - No syntax errors (`testparm` returns 0) --- ## 6. Deprecation Window for Private Helpers ### A. No API Changes — Only Internal Refactor - All private helpers (`_set_smb_password`, `_write_systemd_dropin`, `_share_exists_in_testparm`, `_systemctl_is_active`) remain **unchanged in signature or behavior**. - Only their *implementation* changed (replaced direct `subprocess.run` with `run_sudo`). ### B. Deprecation of *Old* Internal Patterns (Not Public APIs) | Old Pattern | New Pattern | Deprecation Action | |-------------|-------------|--------------------| | `subprocess.run(["sudo", "smbpasswd", ...], input=...)` | `_set_smb_password(user, password)` | **No deprecation needed** — function interface unchanged | | `subprocess.run(["tee", "-a", ...], input=...)` | `run_sudo(["tee", "-a", ...], input=...)` | **No deprecation needed** — `run_sudo` now supports `input=` | | `CommandResult.returncode == -1` | `result.is_timeout` / `result.is_not_found` | **No deprecation needed** — `smb.py` still uses `result.success` (backward compatible) | > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Deprecation** > - No `RuntimeError` or warnings emitted for existing callers > - `mesh smb status` runs without error > - No `DeprecationWarning` in logs --- ## 7. Golden-File Validation: Verifying Identical Behavior ### A. Unit-Level: `test_smb.py` - Existing tests (§2) already verify: - `_set_smb_password` handles passwords identically - `_share_exists_in_testparm` matches `testparm -s` output - `_write_systemd_dropin` creates identical content ### B. Integration-Level: `smb.conf` Diff Check Create a test that runs `setup_server` on a *clean VM* before/after refactor and compares: ```bash # Run on clean Ubuntu VM mesh smb setup-server --share shared --path /tmp/shared --password test123 cp /etc/samba/smb.conf /tmp/smb.conf.after # Revert to old SHA, run again git checkout <old_sha> uv sync --locked mesh smb setup-server --share shared --path /tmp/shared --password test123 cp /etc/samba/smb.conf /tmp/smb.conf.before # Diff diff /tmp/smb.conf.before /tmp/smb.conf.after && echo "CONFIG IDENTICAL" ``` > ✅ **Go/No-Go for Integration Test** > - `diff` shows *no differences* in `smb.conf` content > - `testparm -s` validates both configs identically > - `systemctl status smbd` shows active in both cases ### C. Service State Comparison | Check | Command | Expected | |-------|---------|----------| | Share exists | `grep -A6 "\[shared\]" /etc/samba/smb.conf` | Identical lines | | SMB user exists | `sudo pdbedit -L | grep testuser` | Same UID, no extra users | | Service active | `systemctl is-active smbd` | `active` | | UFW rule added | `sudo ufw status | grep samba` | `Samba Full` (if UFW active) | --- ## Final Go/No-Go Checklist | Stage | Go Criteria | No-Go Criteria | |-------|-------------|----------------| | **Feature Branch** | All unit tests pass; lint/type-check clean | Any test failure or `mypy` error | | **Dev Workstations** | `mesh smb status` + `setup-client` produce *identical* output to baseline | Any new warnings, prompts, or PowerShell differences | | **Production** | `mesh smb setup-server` succeeds; `smb.conf` unchanged; `testparm` validates | Any `smb.conf` corruption, service failure, or config drift | | **Rollback Ready** | `uv sync --locked` pins to old SHA in <5 min | No `pyproject.toml` rollback path or missing backup | --- **Approval Sign-off** - [ ] Dev workstation smoke test passed (`mesh smb status` + `setup-client`) - [ ] `smb.conf` golden-file diff shows *no changes* - [ ] Rollback procedure tested on staging VM - [ ] `pyproject.toml` updated in CI with `v2.1.0-rc1` SHA **Ready for merge to `main` and prod rollout** ✅
