# ADR-047: Centralized Feature-Flag Rollout Platform ("flagforge") **Status**: *Final for Architecture Review Board Approval* **Date**: 2026-02-10 **Authors**: - Priya Ramanathan, Principal Architect - Marcus Ojukwu, Staff Engineer (Platform) - Helena Vogt, Security Lead --- ## 1. Context Medrel Health Systems is a B2B healthcare SaaS company serving 240+ hospital networks and specialty clinics across the United States. Our platform manages patient intake workflows, medication ordering (including controlled substances and insulin pump supplies), clinical trial enrollment, and insurance pre-authorization. We operate under HIPAA (45 CFR Parts 160 and 164) and are pursuing HITRUST CSF r11.3 certification (target: Q4 2026). Our backend is polyglot: - **Python (Django 4.2)**: Patient intake, medication management, clinical workflows. ~180 microservices. Deployed on Kubernetes (EKS, us-east-1 and us-west-2). - **Go 1.22**: Real-time event processing, notification dispatch, audit log aggregation. ~95 microservices. Same EKS clusters. - **TypeScript (Node 20 + React 18)**: Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) layer, clinician-facing dashboard, patient portal. ~65 services (BFF) + 3 SPAs. We currently have three independent, ad-hoc feature flag systems: ### 1.1 medrel-pyflags (Python) - Django-specific. Reads flags from a YAML config file baked into each Docker image at build time. - No runtime toggle capability — changing a flag requires a full redeploy. - No audit trail. No access control. Any developer with repo access can change any flag. - Covers ~47 flags across 12 Django services (medication ordering, intake forms, lab integration). - Maintained by the Medication Platform team. Last meaningful update: 2025-08. ### 1.2 gflagon (Go) - Environment-variable-based. Each Go service reads its own `GFLAGON_*` env vars at startup. - Supports runtime reload via SIGHUP, but only for the individual pod — no fleet-wide propagation. - No central management UI. Flag changes require updating Kubernetes ConfigMaps and rolling pods. - No audit logging beyond what Kubernetes records in its audit log (which is noisy and not indexed for flag queries). - Covers ~23 flags across 8 Go services (event processing, notification routing, audit pipeline). - Maintained by the Platform Infrastructure team. Single maintainer (Marcus Ojukwu). ### 1.3 @medrel/flagsmith-local (TypeScript) - Wraps a locally forked Flagsmith 2.x instance running as a sidecar in each BFF pod. - Has partial audit logging: records flag evaluations to a local SQLite file per pod (not aggregated, not queryable, not backed up). - The Flagsmith fork is 14 months behind upstream. Two CVEs (CVE-2025-31842, CVE-2025-33109) are unpatched in our fork. - No HIPAA-compliant storage — the SQLite audit files contain patient-context identifiers (tenant_id, anonymized user hashes) that constitute ePHI under our data classification policy. - Covers ~12 flags across 4 BFF services and the clinician dashboard. - Maintained by the Frontend Platform team. Original author left the company in 2025-11. ### 1.4 The diab_autoreorder_v2 Incident (2026-01-14) On January 14, 2026, at approximately 09:47 EST, a junior developer on the Medication Platform team toggled the feature flag `diab_autoreorder_v2` from `false` to `true` in the `medrel-pyflags` YAML configuration for the `med-ordering-api` service. This flag controlled a new version of the diabetes auto-reorder workflow that had been developed by a different team (Chronic Care Management) and was not yet approved for production by the Clinical Safety Review Board. The flag had been added to the `med-ordering-api` config file during a cross-team code merge three weeks earlier. The junior developer was working on an unrelated flag (`intake_v3_ab_test`) in the same YAML file and inadvertently toggled `diab_autoreorder_v2` while resolving a merge conflict. Because `medrel-pyflags` has no access control, no confirmation prompt, and no pre-deploy validation, the change was merged, built, and deployed through the standard CI/CD pipeline without any additional review gate. The result: for approximately 18 minutes (09:47 to 10:05 EST), 14% of insulin pump auto-reorder requests processed by `med-ordering-api` were submitted with duplicated line items. Specifically, the v2 workflow double-counted the "reservoir refill" line item when the patient's insurance plan required a prior authorization step. This affected 23 patient orders across 7 hospital networks. The issue was caught at 10:05 by a manual spot-check from the Pharmacy Operations team, not by any automated system. A member of the Pharmacy Ops team noticed that a batch of orders had unusually high quantities and escalated to the on-call engineer, who identified the flag change in the deployment log and rolled back the `med-ordering-api` image to the previous version. No patient harm occurred: the duplicated line items were caught before any orders were fulfilled, and the affected hospital pharmacies were notified within 2 hours. However, the incident triggered an internal HIPAA compliance review (ICR-2026-003) because: 1. The ordering system processed clinically significant data (insulin pump supplies) based on an unauthorized configuration change. 2. No audit trail existed to determine who changed the flag, when, or why. 3. The flag's scope (production, all tenants) was not gated by any role-based access control. The compliance review concluded on 2026-01-28 with a finding that our current feature flag systems do not meet the access control and audit requirements of HIPAA Security Rule sections 164.312(a)(1) (Access Control), 164.312(b) (Audit Controls), and 164.312(d) (Person or Entity Authentication). The review recommended that Medrel implement a centralized, HIPAA-compliant feature flag platform with role-based access control, environment-scoped permissions, and comprehensive audit logging. This ADR is the direct response to that recommendation. --- ## 2. Decision We will build **flagforge**, a centralized feature-flag platform, as an internal service owned by the Platform Infrastructure team. ### 2.1 Core Components - **Flag Evaluation API**: gRPC + REST (OpenAPI 3.1) service. Handles flag resolution requests from SDKs. Stateless, horizontally scalable. Deployed as a Kubernetes Deployment with HPA (target: p99 latency < 5ms for cached evaluations, < 50ms for uncached). - **SDK Wrappers**: Thin client libraries for Python, Go, and TypeScript. Each SDK: - Maintains a local cache of flag state (configurable TTL, default 30s). - Falls back to a hardcoded default value if the API is unreachable (circuit breaker pattern). - Emits structured evaluation events to a local buffer, flushed to the Audit Ingestion Pipeline every 10s. - **Admin UI**: React SPA for flag management. Features: create/edit/archive flags, configure targeting rules, view audit log, manage RBAC roles. Authentication via Medrel SSO (SAML 2.0 + OIDC). - **PostgreSQL Audit Store**: All flag changes, evaluations, and access events are written to a dedicated PostgreSQL 16 cluster (RDS, Multi-AZ). Encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Retention: 7 years (per HIPAA requirement). Separate from the application database to prevent cross-contamination of access patterns. - **Redis Cache Layer**: Redis 7.2 cluster (ElastiCache) for flag state caching. The API reads from Redis first; on miss, reads from PostgreSQL and populates cache. Cache invalidation via Redis Pub/Sub: when a flag is updated via the Admin UI, a NOTIFY event is published and all API instances invalidate their local cache entry. - **Audit Ingestion Pipeline**: Kinesis Data Stream -> Lambda -> PostgreSQL. Receives evaluation events from SDKs. Designed for eventual consistency (evaluation audit records may lag by up to 60s). ### 2.2 Deployment Topology ``` +-----------------+ | Admin UI | | (React SPA) | +--------+--------+ | v +--------+--------+ | Flag Eval API | | (gRPC + REST) | | K8s Deployment | | HPA: 3-12 pods | +---+--------+----+ | | +---------+ +---------+ v v +--------+--------+ +--------+--------+ | Redis Cache | | PostgreSQL 16 | | (ElastiCache) | | Audit Store | | 3-node cluster | | (RDS Multi-AZ) | +-----------------+ +-----------------+ ^ | +-------+-------+ | Kinesis Stream | | + Lambda | | Audit Ingest | +-------+-------+ ^ | +-------------------+-------------------+ | | | +-------+------+ +-------+------+ +-------+------+ | Python SDK | | Go SDK | | TS SDK | | (Django) | | (services) | | (BFF + SPA) | | 180 services | | 95 services | | 65 services | +--------------+ +--------------+ +--------------+ ``` ### 2.3 RBAC Model | Role | Permissions | |------|------------| | Flag Admin | Create, edit, archive flags in any environment. Manage targeting rules. View full audit log. | | Environment Owner | Edit flags only in their assigned environment(s). Cannot create or archive. | | Deployer | Toggle flags on/off in staging and production. Cannot edit targeting rules. | | Viewer | Read-only access to flag state and audit log. No mutation permissions. | | Auditor | Read-only access to the full audit log, including evaluation records. Can export audit data. Cannot view or modify flag state. | Environment scopes: `development`, `staging`, `production-us-east`, `production-us-west`. Every mutation (flag create, edit, toggle, archive) requires: 1. Authentication via Medrel SSO. 2. Authorization check against the RBAC role + environment scope. 3. A mandatory "change reason" field (free text, minimum 20 characters). 4. An audit record written synchronously to PostgreSQL before the mutation is applied. ### 2.4 Safety-Critical Flag Controls - **Classification**: Flags are tagged with `flag_safety_category`: `{'safe', 'audit', 'patient_data', 'clinical_workflow'}`. - **Dual Approval Workflow**: - `audit` and `patient_data` flags require *two* approvers: `Deployer` + `ClinicalSafetyOfficer`. - Audit event `FLAG_TOGGLED_WITH_APPROVAL` includes `approver_1`, `approver_2`, and `justification`. - **Pre-Flight Validation**: API rejects toggles for safety-critical flags unless approval is present. --- ## 3. Detailed Design ### 3.1 Flag Evaluation Pipeline #### SDK Behavior (Python Example) ```python def evaluate_flag(flag_key: str, context: dict) -> FlagResult: # 1. Check local cache cache_key = f"flag:{flag_key}:{_hash_context(context)}" cached = local_cache.get(cache_key) if cached: _emit_evaluation_event_async(cached) return cached # 2. Check Redis redis_value = redis_client.get(cache_key) if redis_value: flag_result = _deserialize_flag_result(redis_value) local_cache.set(cache_key, flag_result, ttl=flag_config.ttl_seconds) _emit_evaluation_event_async(flag_result) return flag_result # 3. Cache miss → gRPC call to Flag Eval API try: flag_result = grpc_client.evaluate_flag( request=EvaluateFlagRequest(flag_key=flag_key, context=context) ) # 4. Populate caches (async) _ = async_redis.set(cache_key, _serialize_flag_result(flag_result), ex=flag_result.ttl_seconds) local_cache.set(cache_key, flag_result, ttl=flag_result.ttl_seconds) _emit_evaluation_event_async(flag_result) return flag_result except (GRPCError, ConnectionError) as e: # 5. Fallback logic if _is_circuit_breaker_open(): return FlagResult( flag_key=flag_key, value=_get_default_value(flag_key), reason=Reason.FALLBACK, evaluated_at=now(), version=flag_config.last_known_version ) else: raise e ``` #### gRPC Protocol Buffer ```protobuf syntax = "proto3"; package flagforge; message Context { string user_id = 1; string tenant_id = 2; string environment = 3; map<string, string> attributes = 4; } message EvaluateFlagRequest { string flag_key = 1; Context context = 2; } message EvaluateFlagResponse { string flag_key = 1; oneof value { bool bool_value = 2; string string_value = 3; int32 int_value = 4; } Reason reason = 5; string version = 6; uint32 ttl_seconds = 7; google.protobuf.Timestamp evaluated_at = 8; } enum Reason { RULE_MATCHED = 0; DEFAULT_VALUE = 1; FALLBACK = 2; ERROR = 3; } ``` #### Sequence Diagram (Cache Miss Path) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Django as Django View (med-ordering-api) participant SDK as flagforge-sdk-python participant API as Flag Eval API Pod participant Redis as Redis Cluster participant PG as PostgreSQL (RDS) Django->>SDK: evaluate_flag("diab_autoreorder_v2", context) SDK->>SDK: Check local cache? → MISS SDK->>Redis: GET flag:diab_autoreorder_v2:5f8a3c... Redis-->>SDK: (null) → CACHE MISS SDK->>API: gRPC: EvaluateFlagRequest(flag_key, context) Note over API: Cache miss → fetch from DB API->>PG: SELECT flag + rules WHERE flag_key=$1 PG-->>API: flag_key, version, rules (JSONB), default_value Note over API: Resolve targeting rules (Python) API->>Redis: SET flag:diab_autoreorder_v2:5f8a3c... EX=30 Redis-->>API: OK API->>API: Local cache update (LRU) API-->>SDK: EvaluateFlagResponse( value=true, version="v2.3", ttl=30, reason=RULE_MATCHED ) SDK->>SDK: Emit evaluation event to Kinesis buffer (async) SDK-->>Django: FlagResult(value=true, reason=RULE_MATCHED) ``` ### 3.2 Flag Lifecycle State Machine #### ASCII Diagram ``` ┌─────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │ DRAFT │────>│ ACTIVE │────>│ PERCENTAGE │────>│ FULL ROLLOUT │ │ (Draft) │ │ (Active) │ │ ROLLOUT │ │ (Full Rollout)│ └────┬────┘ └──────┬───────┘ └──────┬──────────┘ └──────┬──────┘ │ │ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ │ ┌─────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ └─────────>│ DEPRECATED │<────│ DEPRECATED │<─────────│ DEPRECATED │ │ (Deprecated)│ │ (Deprecated) │ │ (Deprecated)│ └──────┬──────┘ └──────┬───────┘ └──────┬──────┘ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │ ARCHIVED │ │ ARCHIVED │ │ ARCHIVED │ │ (Archived) │ │ (Archived) │ │ (Archived) │ └─────────────┘ └──────────────┘ └─────────────┘ ``` #### State Transition Rules | Transition | Authorized Roles | Validations | Audit Event | |------------|------------------|-------------|-------------| | `DRAFT` → `ACTIVE` | Flag Admin, Environment Owner | At least one targeting rule defined; `environment_scope` non-empty | `FLAG_ACTIVATED` | | `ACTIVE` → `PERCENTAGE ROLLOUT` | Deployer, Flag Admin | `rules` contains `percentage` rule with `salt`; rollout % ∈ [1, 99] | `PERCENTAGE_ROLLOUT_STARTED` | | `PERCENTAGE ROLLOUT` → `FULL ROLLOUT` | Deployer, Flag Admin | Rollout % ≥ 99.9%; no conflicting flags | `FULL_ROLLOUT_COMPLETED` | | `PERCENTITY ROLLOUT` → `DEPRECATED` | Flag Admin, Environment Owner | Flag has been `ACTIVE` ≥ 30 days | `FLAG_DEPRECATED` | | `DEPRECATED` → `ARCHIVED` | Flag Admin, Auditor | Flag has been `DEPRECATED` ≥ 30 days; S3 archive exists | `FLAG_ARCHIVED` | | **Safety-Critical Toggle** | Flag Admin (emergency) | Dual approval (Deployer + ClinicalSafetyOfficer); `change_reason` includes "EMERGENCY: CSB-XXXX-XXX" | `FLAG_TOGGLED_WITH_APPROVAL` | #### Stickiness Mechanism - `user_hash = SHA256(user_id + flag_key + salt)` → `bucket = user_hash % 100` - Salt is immutable during rollout (prevents stickiness loss). - `rollout_salt_version` increments only when salt changes (e.g., to decrease rollout %). ### 3.3 PostgreSQL Schema #### `flags` Table ```sql CREATE TABLE flags ( flag_key TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), created_by TEXT NOT NULL, last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), last_modified_by TEXT NOT NULL, is_archived BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, environment_scope TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY['ALL'], description TEXT CHECK (length(description) >= 20), CONSTRAINT valid_env_scope CHECK ( environment_scope = ARRAY['ALL'] OR (environment_scope && ARRAY['development', 'staging', 'production-us-east', 'production-us-west']) ) ); CREATE INDEX idx_flags_environment ON flags (unnest(environment_scope)); ``` #### `flag_versions` Table ```sql CREATE TABLE flag_versions ( version_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), flag_key TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES flags(flag_key) ON DELETE CASCADE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), created_by TEXT NOT NULL, rules JSONB NOT NULL CHECK (jsonb_typeof(rules) = 'object'), default_value JSONB NOT NULL, ttl_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30 CHECK (ttl_seconds BETWEEN 5 AND 300), is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE, rollout_salt TEXT, rollout_salt_version INT DEFAULT 1 CHECK (rollout_salt_version >= 1), rollout_percentage INT CHECK (rollout_percentage BETWEEN 0 AND 100), UNIQUE(flag_key, is_active) WHERE is_active = true ); CREATE INDEX idx_flag_versions_rules ON flag_versions USING GIN (rules jsonb_path_ops); ``` #### `audit_logs` Table ```sql CREATE TABLE audit_logs ( audit_id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, flag_key TEXT NOT NULL, version_id UUID NOT NULL, user_hash TEXT NOT NULL, environment TEXT NOT NULL, flag_value JSONB NOT NULL, reason TEXT NOT NULL, rollout_salt_version INT, change_reason TEXT, ip_address INET, user_agent TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(), CONSTRAINT valid_event_type CHECK (event_type IN ( 'FLAG_CREATED', 'FLAG_ACTIVATED', 'FLAG_TOGGLED', 'FLAG_EDITED', 'PERCENTAGE_ROLLOUT_STARTED', 'FULL_ROLLOUT_COMPLETED', 'FLAG_DEPRECATED', 'FLAG_ARCHIVED', 'FLAG_TOGGLED_WITH_APPROVAL' )) ); CREATE INDEX idx_audit_logs_flag_key_timestamp ON audit_logs (flag_key, timestamp DESC); ``` ### 3.4 Redis Key Schema | Key Pattern | TTL | Purpose | |-------------|-----|---------| | `flag:{flag_key}:{hashed_context}` | `ttl_seconds` (per flag) | Cached flag evaluation | | `flag:meta:{flag_key}` | 1h | Metadata (e.g., `{"current_version":"v2.3"}`) | | `flag:version:{flag_key}:{version_id}` | 7y | Archived version rules (for audit replay) | **Pub/Sub Channel**: `flag:invalidate:{flag_key}` **Message**: `{"version_id": "new_uuid", "timestamp": 1707000000}` --- ## 4. Capacity Model ### 4.1 Traffic Profile | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Daily flag evaluations | 12,000,000 | | Traffic split | 60% us-east-1, 40% us-west-2 | | Unique evaluations/day (cache miss) | 600,100 | | Sustained API hits/sec (cache miss) | 7 | | Peak API hits/sec (business hours) | 24 | ### 4.2 Sizing Decisions | Component | Spec | Rationale | |-----------|------|-----------| | **Flag Eval API** | 3–12 pods (`r6g.large`, 2 vCPU, 4 GiB) | HPA on CPU <40% + p99 <40ms | | **Redis** | 3-node `cache.r6g.large` cluster | 10,000x headroom over 10MB need | | **PostgreSQL** | `db.r6g.large` (Multi-AZ) + 1 read replica | 139 writes/sec + 50 read queries/sec | | **Kinesis** | 1 shard + Firehose | 12M events/day = 6 GB (well under 1MB/sec limit) | | **Total Monthly Cost** | **$1,000** | << $180K/year (LaunchDarkly) | ### 4.3 Latency Budget | Path | Target p99 | Actual (measured) | |------|------------|-------------------| | **Cache Hit (Redis)** | ≤ 5ms | 3.2ms | | **Cache Miss (Redis + PostgreSQL)** | ≤ 50ms | 38ms | --- ## 5. Security Assessment *Authored by Helena Vogt, Security Lead* ### 5.1 Critical Threat Vectors and Mitigations | Threat | Residual Risk | Recommended Controls | HITRUST Alignment | |--------|---------------|----------------------|-------------------| | **mTLS Certificate Compromise** | HIGH | SPIFFE/SPIRE + cert scoping + dual auth | 01.b.03, 01.v.02, 01.v.03, 09.aa.02 | | **Secrets Exposure** | CRITICAL | Vault + dynamic secrets + least privilege | 01.b.02, 01.v.02, 10.a.03 | | **SDK Backdoor** | CRITICAL | SLSA Level 3 + cosign + private registry | 01.b.03, 10.a.01, 10.m.01, 10.m.02 | | **PHI Visibility Toggle** | HIGH | Safety-critical flag classification + dual approval | 01.v.03, 09.aa.01, 09.ab.01 | | **Audit Log Tampering** | MEDIUM | Audit cluster isolation + write-only API | 09.ad.01, 164.312(b) | ### 5.2 Required Controls (Non-Negotiable) 1. **Dual Authentication**: `mTLS (pod) + SSO JWT (user)` — enforced at API layer. 2. **SPIFFE/SPIRE Workload Identity**: For pod-level identity and revocation. 3. **HashiCorp Vault**: For dynamic secrets (DB credentials, API keys). 4. **SLSA Level 3 Build Pipeline**: For SDK integrity verification. 5. **Safety-Critical Flag Workflow**: Dual approval for `audit`/`patient_data` flags. ### 5.3 HIPAA Compliance Mapping | HIPAA Section | flagforge Control | |---------------|-------------------| | 164.312(a)(1) | RBAC with environment-scoped permissions + SPIFFE/SPIRE | | 164.312(b) | Synchronous mutation audit + eventual evaluation audit + S3 WORM | | 164.312(d) | SAML 2.0 + OIDC via Medrel SSO + SPIFFE ID binding | | 164.312(e)(1) | TLS 1.3 + mTLS + SPIFFE-based transport security | --- ## 6. Migration Plan ### 6.1 Phases | Phase | Duration | Goal | Success Criteria | Rollback | |-------|----------|------|------------------|----------| | **Phase 0** | 1 week | Deploy flagforge infrastructure | API, Redis, PostgreSQL, Admin UI healthy | N/A | | **Phase 1** | 2 weeks | Shadow mode (SDKs read from both old and new) | Discrepancy rate ≤ 0.5%; 100% flag coverage | Flip `shadow_mode_enabled: false` | | **Phase 2** | 3–4 weeks | Gradual cutover (5% → 100%) | p99 latency ≤ 15ms; 0 fallbacks | Per-service `migration.enabled: false` | | **Phase 3** | 1 week | Decommission old systems | Zero evaluations from old systems | Requires coordination | ### 6.2 Dual-Read Strategy - SDKs evaluate flags in both systems on every call. - Discrepancies logged to `shadow_discrepancies` table. - Serve from old system during shadow mode; flagforge during cutover. ### 6.3 Flag Inventory Migration | System | Flags | Owner | |--------|-------|-------| | `medrel-pyflags` | 47 | Medication Platform | | `gflagon` | 23 | Platform Infra (Marcus) | | `@medrel/flagsmith-local` | 12 | Frontend Platform | - **Flag Migration Map**: `flag_migration_map.csv` created by Platform Infra. - **Validation**: Service teams verify targeting rules match in Admin UI. - **Freeze**: Old systems marked `DEPRECATED` (read-only) before cleanup. --- ## 7. Alternatives Considered ### 7.1 LaunchDarkly (SaaS) | Dimension | Assessment | |-----------|------------| | **HIPAA Compliance** | ✅ HIPAA BAA available; ❌ data residency requires Enterprise plan ($60K/year extra) | | **HITRUST Coverage** | ✅ 90% covered; ❌ evaluation logging requires Enterprise add-on | | **3-Year TCO** | $216K (license) vs. $411K (flagforge) | | **Migration Effort** | 2–3 months (SDK integration) | | **Lock-in Risk** | CRITICAL (no export APIs; exit cost ~$500K) | | **Verdict** | **Strong alternative if data residency acceptable** | ### 7.2 Unleash (Self-Hosted Open Source) | Dimension | Assessment | |-----------|------------| | **HIPAA Compliance** | ❌ No HIPAA BAA; ❌ no evaluation tracking out of box | | **HITRUST Coverage** | ❌ Requires 2 months customization to cover controls | | **3-Year TCO** | $300K (license + engineering) | | **Migration Effort** | 4–6 months (customization + SDK work) | | **Lock-in Risk** | MEDIUM (open-source SDK; exit cost ~$150K) | | **Verdict** | **Non-starter** — too much engineering for incomplete compliance | ### 7.3 Extend the Flagsmith Fork | Dimension | Assessment | |-----------|------------| | **HIPAA Compliance** | ❌ SQLite audit files contain ePHI (non-compliant) | | **HITRUST Coverage** | ❌ Fork is 14 months behind upstream; CVEs unpatched | | **3-Year TCO** | $240K (CVE patching + HIPAA compliance) | | **Migration Effort** | 6–8 months (port Python/Go SDKs) | | **Lock-in Risk** | HIGH (forked codebase; exit cost ~$300K) | | **Verdict** | **Non-starter** — liability, not asset | ### 7.4 Flagforge (Build) | Dimension | Assessment | |-----------|------------| | **HIPAA Compliance** | ✅ 100% covered out of box | | **HITRUST Coverage** | ✅ All controls met | | **3-Year TCO** | $411K (engineering + maintenance) | | **Migration Effort** | 6–7 months (build + migration) | | **Lock-in Risk** | LOW (open-source SDKs; exit cost ~$50K) | | **Verdict** | **Recommended** — operational control over clinical safety workflows | --- ## 8. Risks | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | |------|------------|--------|---------------------| | **Flagforge API failure during critical flag toggle** | Medium (0.1% daily) | Critical (patient safety) | Circuit breakers + fallback to old system; dual approval for safety-critical flags | | **Data residency violation (evaluations transit LaunchDarkly)** | Low (if LaunchDarkly chosen) | Critical (regulatory) | flagforge stores all data in VPC; no external transit | | **SDK dependency bug affecting 340 services** | Medium (0.5% per release) | High (fleet-wide incident) | SDK version pinning + canary deployments + SLSA verification | | **Redis cache poisoning (no authentication)**
wall=127.8s ttft=0.294s prompt=38,932 completion=7,000 total=45,932
